title: Distinct lattice and charge excitations in AV3Sb5 kagome superconductors
authors: Dongjin Oh, Stefan Enzner, Lennart Klebl, Harley D. Scammell, Julian Ingham, Tim Wehling, Giorgio Sangiovanni, Ronny Thomale, Ahmet Kemal Demir, Connor Occhialini, Dirk Wulferding, Ziqiang Wang, Andrea C. Salinas, Stephen D. Wilson, Riccardo Comin
abstract: The kagome superconductor family AV3Sb5 (A=Cs, Rb, K) provides a rich platform for exploring diverse electronic symmetry breaking phenomena, including superconductivity and various forms of density wave orders. Although these compounds share the identical lattice structure in the normal state, they exhibit distinct forms of symmetry breaking upon entering the charge density wave (CDW) phase, and the microscopic origin of which remain elusive. Here, we investigate the lattice and charge degrees of freedom in AV3Sb5 using angle-resolved polarized Raman spectroscopy. Our comprehensive polarization-resolved measurements reveal that the lifting of the twofold-degeneracy of the E2g phonon mode in the CDW phase-previously reported only in CsV3Sb5 with a 3 GHz splitting-also appears ubiquitously in the other two compounds. In contrast, the collective CDW excitations exhibit markedly different polarization dependences depending on the alkali-metal species. These distinct behaviors in the lattice and charge channels provide crucial insight into the enigmatic material-dependent symmetry breaking phenomena that appear in the CDW phase. Furthermore, our experiments, together with first-principles calculations and an effective Hamiltonian model, shed light on the nature of the charge order structure in AV3Sb5 kagome superconductors.
summary: This paper investigates the difference between lattice and charge excitations in AV3Sb5 kagome superconductors using polarized Raman spectroscopy. It finds that while the splitting of certain phonon modes is a universal feature across Cs, Rb, and K variants, the charge density wave excitations are highly material-dependent. This distinction provides new clues into how symmetry breaking emerges in these correlated electronic systems.
main_problem: The microscopic origin of the distinct electronic symmetry-breaking phenomena and the material-dependent charge density wave (CDW) orders in AV3Sb5 (A=Cs, Rb, K) kagome superconductors remains elusive.
main_result: The study reveals that while the lifting of the E2g phonon degeneracy is a ubiquitous lattice anomaly across all three compounds, the collective CDW amplitude modes exhibit markedly different polarization dependences depending on the alkali-metal species.
method: Angle-resolved polarized Raman spectroscopy (ARPRS) combined with first-principles calculations (DFPT) and an effective Hamiltonian model.
model_or_system: The physical system is the family of AV3Sb5 (A=Cs, Rb, K) kagome superconductors, which feature both superconductivity and charge density wave phases with complex symmetry breaking.
key_observables: Phonon modes (E2g doublet splitting and A1g singlet), CDW amplitude modes, and polarization-resolved Raman spectra (XX and XY geometries).
important_parameters: Polarization angle (theta), temperature (T), Raman shift (cm-1), and phonon frequency modulation.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes the observed phonon shifts are due to spectral overlap of independent modes rather than experimental misalignment.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows the experimental geometry and basic Raman spectra; Fig 2 demonstrates the fourfold modulation of the P1 phonon frequency across Cs, Rb, and K compounds; Fig 3 provides detailed Lorentzian fitting of the P1 phonon splitting.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of symmetry breaking in AV3Sb5, presents experimental Raman spectroscopy results showing universal lattice anomalies and distinct charge dynamics, and concludes with theoretical modeling and implications for charge order structures.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) Experimental geometry of polarization-resolved Raman spectroscopy. Green and red arrows indicate incident and scattered lights, respectively. (b) Phonon spectrum of CsV3Sb5 measured in parallel polarization (XX) geometry at 5 K. (c) CDW amplitude mode spectrum of CsV3Sb5 mea- sured in XX geometry at 5 K.
FIG. 2. Polarization angle (θ)-dependent phonon frequencies of AV3Sb5. (a) θ-dependence of the P1 phonon frequency in CsV3Sb5. (b) Raw P1 phonon spectra measured at θ = 130◦

FIG. 3. P1 phonon splitting of AV3Sb5 in CDW phase (a,b) Two Lorentzian function ﬁtting results of XX Raman spectra of CsV3Sb5 measured at θ = 130◦(a) and θ = 180◦(b). The green and orange shaded areas indicate the spectral weights of the P1(1) and P1(2) phonons, respectively. The red solid curves and circles correspond to the integrated spectral weights of P1(1) and P1(2) phonons and the raw Raman data, respectively. (c) θ-dependent spectral weights of the P1(1) and P1(2) phonons, extracted from XX Raman spectra. Circles and solid curves represent experimental data and simulated results, respectively. (d-f) Corresponding plots for the XY Raman spectra of CsV3Sb5. All data were collected...

FIG. 4. Amplitude mode spectra of AV3Sb5. (a) Angle-resolved polarized Raman spectra of CsV3Sb5. (b) Raw Raman spectra of CsV3Sb5. Red, blue and green curves correspond to measurements at polarization angles (θ) of 0◦, 45◦, and 90◦, respectively. Dashed lines indicate the peak positions of the amplitude modes. (c-f) Corresponding plots for RbV3Sb5 (c and d) and KV3Sb5 (e and f), respectively. The θ-dependent Raman spectra were measured in the XX polarization geometry with 2.33 eV photon energy.
